# JobWeekly App — CLAUDE.md

This file documents the architecture, patterns, and conventions for the JobWeekly app. It is intended to be read by AI agents to quickly orient themselves before making changes.

---

## Project Overview

**JobWeekly** (getjobweekly.com) is a subscription SaaS for job seekers. Paying subscribers receive a personalized weekly job search report generated by AI: curated job recommendations, networking leads, resume tailoring tips, and application tracking. The $9.99/week subscription is managed via Stripe. Users onboard by uploading a resume and setting job preferences; reports are auto-regenerated every 3 days via a cron job.

---

## Tech Stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 16 (App Router, React 19, TypeScript)
- **Database**: SQLite via [Turso](https://turso.tech) (libSQL), accessed through Drizzle ORM
- **Payments**: Stripe (Checkout Sessions, Subscriptions, Webhooks)
- **AI**: OpenAI API (report generation, resume parsing)
- **File Storage**: Vercel Blob (resume PDFs)
- **Job Search API**: JSearch (RapidAPI) for live job listings
- **Notifications**: Resend (email), Twilio (SMS)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4
- **PDF Generation**: @react-pdf/renderer (tailored resume download)
- **Deployment**: Vercel (serverless, with cron jobs)

---

## App Structure

```
src/app/
  page.tsx                  # Marketing homepage
  layout.tsx                # Root layout
  pricing/                  # Public pricing page
  login/                    # Login form + actions
  signup/                   # Signup form + actions
  onboarding/               # Multi-step profile setup (resume upload, job prefs)
  dashboard/                # User dashboard
  applications/             # Application tracker + report generation trigger
  report/
    weekly/                 # Current weekly report view + refresh action
    [id]/                   # Historical report view
  resume/                   # Resume page
  profile/                  # Profile editing
  settings/                 # Billing, job preferences, referral codes
  billing/                  # Subscription management, referral program
  completed/                # Post-onboarding confirmation
  admin/                    # Owner-only admin panel
    users/                  # User list + per-user management
    contacts/               # Manual networking contact management
    audit/                  # Audit log
  dis-2/                    # Internal tool (Dis-2 data viewer)
  api/
    stripe/webhook/         # Stripe webhook handler
    cron/refresh-reports/   # Scheduled report refresh (called by Vercel cron)
    report/[id]/pdf/        # PDF report download
    resume/download/        # Private resume download (signed Blob URL)
    resume/tailored/        # Tailored resume PDF generation
    refresh-report/         # Manual report refresh endpoint
    dev/reset-password/     # Dev-only password reset utility

src/lib/
  auth/session.ts           # Auth helpers: sessions, requireUser, requireActiveUser, requireOwner
  db/
    index.ts                # Drizzle client setup (Turso / local SQLite)
    schema.ts               # Full DB schema
  payments/stripe.ts        # Lazy Stripe client singleton
  services/
    report-service.ts       # Core report generation logic (OpenAI + JSearch)
    referral-service.ts     # Referral code finalization + bonus week application
  notifications.ts          # Email (Resend) + SMS (Twilio) notification helpers
  pdf/resume-template.tsx   # React PDF template for tailored resume
  manual-contacts.ts        # Loads manually curated networking contacts
  admin-audit.ts            # Admin audit log helpers
  dis2/                     # Dis-2 internal tool types + server logic

src/components/
  layout/app-shell.tsx      # Main authenticated layout shell with nav
  layout/marketing-header.tsx
  ui/                       # Reusable UI primitives (Button, Card, Badge, Progress, etc.)
  report/report-view.tsx    # Report display component
  marketing/                # Landing page section components
```

---

## Database

### Schema Overview (`src/lib/db/schema.ts`)

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `users` | Core user accounts (id, email, passwordHash, firstName, lastName) |
| `profiles` | Extended profile: university, major, GPA, graduation, resume URL/text, LinkedIn, GitHub |
| `jobPreferences` | Target roles, industries, locations, remote preference, dream companies |
| `applications` | Job application tracker (company, role, status, appliedDate, notes) |
| `reports` | Generated weekly reports (id, userId, summary, pdfUrl, status) |
| `reportRecommendations` | Job recommendations within a report |
| `networkingLeads` | Networking contacts within a report |
| `resumeRecommendations` | Resume bullet suggestions within a report |
| `notificationPreferences` | Email/SMS digest schedule and thresholds |
| `subscriptions` | Stripe subscription state (status, stripeSubscriptionId, currentPeriodEnd, bonusWeeksApplied) |
| `sessions` | Auth session tokens (14-day TTL, cascade deletes) |
| `referralCodes` | 6-char hex referral codes; tracks issuer and redeemer |

### DB Client (`src/lib/db/index.ts`)

- Uses `@libsql/client` + `drizzle-orm/libsql`.
- In production: connects to `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` (forced to `https://` transport to avoid WebSocket idle drops on Vercel).
- In local dev: falls back to `file:./data/main.sqlite`.
- Has one inline migration on startup: `ALTER TABLE profiles ADD COLUMN resume_text text` (idempotent, error is swallowed).

### Migrations

```bash
npm run db:generate   # Generate SQL migration files into ./drizzle/
npm run db:push       # Push schema changes directly to the database (no migration file)
```

`drizzle.config.ts` reads `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` and `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN`. For local dev these env vars can be omitted and it uses `file:./data/main.sqlite`.

---

## Auth

All auth lives in `src/lib/auth/session.ts`. It uses custom session tokens stored in the `sessions` table — **no NextAuth, no JWT**.

### Helpers

| Function | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `getSessionUser()` | Reads `omc_session` cookie, validates token against DB, returns user or null |
| `requireUser(next?)` | Calls `getSessionUser()`; redirects to `/login` if not authenticated |
| `requireActiveUser()` | Calls `requireUser()`; in production, also checks subscription is `active` or `trialing`; redirects to `/billing?activate=1` if not |
| `requireOwner()` | Calls `requireUser()`; checks user email is in `OWNER_EMAILS` allowlist; redirects to `/login?next=/admin` if not |

**Important**: `requireActiveUser()` is bypassed in `NODE_ENV=development`. All protected app pages (dashboard, report, applications, settings) call this. The billing and login pages only call `requireUser()`.

- Session TTL: 14 days
- Password hashing: `scrypt` with a random 16-byte salt (Node.js `crypto`)
- Cookie name: `omc_session` (httpOnly, sameSite=lax, secure in production)

---

## Payments & Subscription Lifecycle

### Stripe Setup

The Stripe client is a lazy singleton in `src/lib/payments/stripe.ts`. It throws if `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is missing at call time (not at import time), so missing keys don't crash unrelated pages.

### Checkout Flow

1. User clicks "Activate" on `/billing` or `/settings`.
2. `startCheckoutSession()` server action in `src/app/billing/actions.ts` creates a Stripe Checkout Session in `subscription` mode.
3. `userId` is passed in both `session.metadata` and `subscription_data.metadata` (needed for webhook routing).
4. On success, Stripe redirects to `/billing?checkout=success`. If the webhook has not yet synced, `BillingPolling` component polls until the subscription record goes active.
5. On cancel, redirects to `/billing?checkout=cancel`.

### Webhook (`src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`)

Handles these events:

| Event | Action |
|---|---|
| `checkout.session.completed` | Syncs subscription record; finalizes referral if present |
| `invoice.payment_succeeded` | Re-syncs subscription record (handles renewal) |
| `customer.subscription.updated` | Re-syncs subscription record |
| `customer.subscription.deleted` | Marks subscription canceled |

**Status normalization** (`normalizeStatus`):
- `active` / `trialing` → `"active"`
- `past_due` / `unpaid` / `incomplete` → `"past_due"`
- `canceled` / `incomplete_expired` → `"canceled"`

### Cancellation

`cancelSubscriptionAction()` sets `cancel_at_period_end: true` on Stripe. It does **not** update the local DB status immediately — the webhook (`customer.subscription.updated`) syncs the change. This avoids a race condition where the webhook would overwrite a premature local `"canceled"` status back to `"active"`.

### Subscription Statuses (local DB)

- `pending` — created but not yet paid
- `active` — active (maps to Stripe `active` and `trialing`)
- `past_due` — payment failed
- `canceled` — subscription ended

### Referral Program

- Active subscribers can generate 6-char hex referral codes.
- When a new user signs up with a code and completes checkout, `finalizeReferralIfPresent()` is called.
- Every 3 completed referrals earns the referrer one free week via Stripe trial extension.
- `bonusWeeksApplied` on the subscription record tracks how many bonus weeks have already been applied to avoid double-crediting.

---

## Report Generation

Core logic is in `src/lib/services/report-service.ts`.

- Fetches user profile, job preferences, resume text, and recent applications.
- Queries JSearch API for live job listings matching user's target roles/locations.
- Sends context to OpenAI to generate: job recommendations, networking leads, and resume bullet suggestions.
- Stores results in `reports`, `reportRecommendations`, `networkingLeads`, and `resumeRecommendations` tables.
- `REPORT_TTL_MS` controls how old a report can be before the cron job regenerates it.

### Cron Job (`src/app/api/cron/refresh-reports/route.ts`)

- Called by Vercel cron (via `GET /api/cron/refresh-reports`).
- Protected by `CRON_SECRET` (Vercel sends `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET>`).
- Iterates all `active`/`trialing` subscriptions, refreshes stale reports, sends notifications.
- `maxDuration = 300` (5 minutes) to accommodate sequential per-user processing.

---

## Key Files Reference

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/lib/db/schema.ts` | Single source of truth for all DB tables |
| `src/lib/db/index.ts` | Drizzle client; exports `db` and all schema tables |
| `src/lib/auth/session.ts` | All auth: sessions, password hashing, `requireUser`, `requireActiveUser`, `requireOwner` |
| `src/lib/payments/stripe.ts` | Lazy Stripe singleton |
| `src/lib/services/report-service.ts` | Report generation (OpenAI + JSearch) |
| `src/lib/services/referral-service.ts` | Referral finalization + Stripe trial extension |
| `src/app/billing/actions.ts` | `startCheckoutSession`, `cancelSubscriptionAction`, `createReferralCodeAction` |
| `src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` | Stripe webhook — canonical subscription sync point |
| `src/app/api/cron/refresh-reports/route.ts` | Scheduled report refresh for all active subscribers |
| `src/lib/notifications.ts` | Email (Resend) + SMS (Twilio) helpers |
| `drizzle.config.ts` | Drizzle Kit config (Turso dialect) |

---

## Dev Commands

```bash
npm run dev           # Start local dev server (http://localhost:3000)
npm run build         # Production build
npm run start         # Serve production build
npm run lint          # ESLint

npm run db:generate   # Generate Drizzle migration files from schema
npm run db:push       # Push schema to DB directly (faster for dev, skips migration files)
```

---

## Environment Variables

Copy `.env.example` to `.env.local`. Required variables:

### Stripe
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` | Stripe secret key (`sk_live_...` or `sk_test_...`) |
| `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | Webhook signing secret (`whsec_...`) — get from Stripe Dashboard |
| `STRIPE_WEEKLY_PRICE_ID` | Stripe Price ID for the $9.99/week plan. If omitted, falls back to an inline `price_data` definition |

### App
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `APP_URL` | Full URL of the app (e.g. `https://getjobweekly.com`). Used for Stripe redirect URLs |
| `OWNER_EMAILS` | Comma-separated list of admin emails. These bypass normal auth guards and access `/admin` |
| `CRON_SECRET` | Secret for authenticating cron job calls from Vercel |

### Database
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` | Turso database URL (`libsql://...` or `https://...`). Omit in local dev to use SQLite file |
| `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` | Turso auth token. Not needed for local SQLite |

### AI & Jobs
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API key for report generation and resume parsing |
| `JSEARCH_API_KEY` | JSearch (RapidAPI) key for live job listings |

### Notifications
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `RESEND_API_KEY` | Resend API key for transactional email |
| `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` | Sender email address |
| `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID` | Twilio account SID for SMS |
| `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN` | Twilio auth token |
| `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER` | Twilio phone number |

### Storage
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` | Vercel Blob token for resume PDF storage |

### Internal tools
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `JSONBIN_BIN_URL` | JSONBin URL for Dis-2 internal tool |
| `JSONBIN_API_KEY` | JSONBin API key |

---

## Common Gotchas

1. **`requireActiveUser()` is skipped in dev.** In `NODE_ENV=development`, the subscription check is skipped entirely — all authenticated users can access the app. This means subscription-gating only activates in production/preview.

2. **Webhook is the canonical subscription sync.** Never directly update `subscriptions.status` to `"canceled"` before the Stripe webhook fires — the subsequent webhook event will overwrite it. `cancelSubscriptionAction` intentionally does NOT write to the DB; it only tells Stripe to set `cancel_at_period_end: true`.

3. **Stripe client is lazy.** `getStripeClient()` throws at call time if `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is missing. Do not call it at module load level or it will crash pages that import the module.

4. **OpenAI client is also lazy.** `getOpenAI()` in `report-service.ts` is called inside functions, not at module load. Same reason — avoid crashing unrelated pages.

5. **DB transport: force HTTPS, not WebSockets.** `src/lib/db/index.ts` replaces `libsql://` with `https://` to avoid WebSocket connections that silently die on idle Vercel serverless instances.

6. **DB fallback stub.** If `createClient` fails on module load, a stub `file::memory:` client is created so the module doesn't crash at import time. Queries will still throw — this just prevents boot-time failures from taking down the entire app.

7. **pdf-parse must be dynamically imported.** `pdf-parse` executes on module load and crashes serverless if imported statically. Always use `await import("pdf-parse")` inside async functions.

8. **`userId` must be in Stripe metadata.** Both `checkout.session.metadata.userId` and `subscription_data.metadata.userId` are set during checkout. The webhook falls back to a DB lookup by `stripeSubscriptionId` if metadata is missing (`lookupUserId`), but the metadata path is preferred.

9. **Referral bonus weeks use Stripe trial extension.** Free weeks are applied by setting `trial_end` on the Stripe subscription, which pauses billing. The `bonusWeeksApplied` column prevents double-application. Even if the Stripe API call fails, the DB record is still updated.

10. **Local SQLite file location.** The local dev database is at `./data/main.sqlite` relative to the project root. The `data/` directory may need to be created manually.

11. **`STRIPE_WEEKLY_PRICE_ID` is optional in dev.** If omitted, `startCheckoutSession` falls back to an inline `price_data` object (hardcoded $9.99/week). For production, always set a real Price ID.

12. **Admin access is email-allowlist based.** `requireOwner()` checks `OWNER_EMAILS` env var (comma-separated). There is no DB-level role — access is purely env-config based.
